LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF EVELYN LEE'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL FILE THE PROPOSED THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. THE LIBERAL POLICY IN FAVOR OF AMENDMENT OUTWEIGHS ANY PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT IN ALLOWING AMENDMENT. THE TRIAL DATE OF FEBRUARY 24, 2014 IS VACATED, AND THE NEW TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR JULY 14, 2014. THE DISCOVERY CUTOFF SHALL BE DETERMINED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE NEW TRIAL DATE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

Off Calendar Feb-04-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WENDY H. CHAU, TEL. NO. 539-6116 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs ALYCE LEE and THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Ajudication is off calendar as moot. On January 24, 2013, Plaintiff filed a third amended complaint that moots the instant motion. See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131. Defendant may renew this motion or file a new motion for summary judgment/adjudication directed to the operative pleading. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: WENDY CHAU, ESQ., 415.539.6116, FOR PLAINTIFF EVELYN LEE.
MARIE TRIMBLE HOLVICK, ESQ., 415.986.5900, FOR DEFENDANTS UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling WITH SLIGHT MODIFICATION: PLAINTIFF EVELYN LEE's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint IS Granted. Plaintiff shall file the proposed Third Amended Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. The liberal policy in favor of amendment outweighs any prejudice to defendant in allowing amendment. The trial date of February 24, 2014 is vacated, and the new trial date is set for JULY 14, 2014. The discovery cutoff shall be determined in accordance with the new trial date. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185
